channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: The Supreme Court will hear arguments over Tennessee's ban on gender-affirming care for minors
item.description: The Supreme Court is set to hear arguments on the constitutionality of Tennessee's ban on gender-affirming care for transgender minors.
item.dc:creator: Mark Sherman And Geoff Mulvihill, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Donald Trump's call for 'energy dominance' is likely to run into real-world limits
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump is set to create a National Energy Council that he says will establish American “energy dominance” around the world as he seeks to boost U.S. oil and gas drilling and move away from President Joe Biden’s focus on climate change.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW DALY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Massachusetts lawmakers push for an effort to ban all tobacco sales over time
item.description: A handful of Massachusetts lawmakers are hoping to persuade their colleagues to support a proposal that would make the state the first to adopt a ban meant to eliminate the use of tobacco products over time.
item.dc:creator: Steve Leblanc, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Texas could bus migrants directly to ICE for deportation instead of sanctuary cities under proposed plan
item.description: Texas could bus migrants to ICE instead of sanctuary cities so they can be processed for deportation faster under a proposed plan, according to media reports.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 7 most competitive Senate races in 2026
item.description: The race for key Senate contests in 2026 is already getting underway, with a few major potential candidates hinting they may join the race.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: What is Evacuation Day? The forgotten holiday that predates Thanksgiving
item.description: Before Thanksgiving became the prominent late November holiday, much of America celebrated "Evacuation Day" – commemorating the day the last Redcoats fled New York in 1783.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Zuckerberg dines with Trump in Mar-a-Lago
item.description: FORT LAUDERDALE, Fla. (AP) — Donald Trump dined on Wednesday with Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg at the president-elect’s Mar-a-Lago club in Florida, bringing together the Facebook founder and the former president who was once banned from that social network.
item.dc:creator: ADRIANA GOMEZ LICON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump claims a win on immigration after a call with Mexico's president. But she suggests no change
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump is declaring a win on stopping illegal immigration through Mexico after talking with that country’s leader.
item.dc:creator: Fatima Hussein, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: US sanctions more allies of Maduro over alleged post-election crackdown in Venezuela
item.description: CARACAS, Venezuela (AP) — The United States has imposed sanctions on an additional 21 allies of Venezuelan President Nicolás Maduro, accusing them on Wednesday of perpetrating some of the repression with which officials in the country responded to July's disputed presidential election.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Americans' opinion of Republican Party on the rise
item.description: In the poll, 45 percent of Americans said they feel “favorable” toward the Republican Party, up 6 points from an Economist/YouGov poll in late October, when 39 percent said they felt favorable toward GOP.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, NewsNation

item.title: Republican Miller-Meeks wins reelection after recount in close Iowa congressional race
item.description: DES MOINES, Iowa (AP) — Republican Rep. Mariannette Miller-Meeks won her reelection bid Wednesday after a recount confirmed her lead, helping her party pad its thin majority in the U.S. House and retain control of all four of Iowa’s congressional seats.
item.dc:creator: HANNAH FINGERHUT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: No Labels pushes back after opposition to third-party run
item.description: No Labels, a centrist group, claims in court documents that some Democrats worked to oppose a third-party candidate
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, NewsNation

item.title: Trump selects longtime adviser Keith Kellogg as special envoy for Ukraine and Russia
item.description: WEST PALM BEACH, Fla. (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump said Wednesday that he has chosen Keith Kellogg, a highly decorated retired three-star general, to serve as his special envoy for Ukraine and Russia.
item.dc:creator: LOLITA C. BALDOR and FATIMA HUSSEIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ohio governor signs 'bathroom bill' into law, restricting students from using opposite-sex restrooms
item.description: Republican Ohio Gov. Mike DeWine has signed a bill into law that will prevent schools from allowing students of the opposite sex into restrooms and locker rooms.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: DeWine signs bill restricting bathroom access for transgender Ohio students
item.description: Transgender students may use single-occupancy or faculty facilities with “controlled use,” according to the bill, Senate Bill 104.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Migdon, NewsNation

item.title: Harris never led Trump, internal polls showed — but DNC officials were kept in the dark
item.description: A top adviser to Kamala Harris said internal campaign polls never saw her beating Trump, despite her public polling posture.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Las Vegas Mayor Carolyn Goodman prepares to leave office, ending an era
item.description: Carolyn Goodman, who has served as Mayor of Las Vegas for 13 years, was honored with the renaming of Civic Plaza in her honor, and looks forward to the future with her husband Oscar.
item.dc:creator: John Langeler, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump nominees targeted in series of threats, spokesperson says
item.description: (The Hill) -- Multiple nominees and appointees tapped to serve in President-elect Trump’s incoming administration were targeted with threats this week, a spokesperson for the transition said Wednesday, though it was unclear how many were affected.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump team says Israel-Hezbollah ceasefire deal brokered by Biden is actually Trump's win
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Biden administration kept President-elect Donald Trump's incoming administration closely apprised of its efforts to broker the ceasefire deal between Israel and Hezbollah that took effect early Wednesday, according to the outgoing Democratic administration.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ranked-choice advocates forced to regroup after election losses
item.description: Proponents of ranked-choice voting are recalibrating after Americans across the country broadly rejected measures intended to implement the system.
item.dc:creator: Julia Mueller, NewsNation

item.title: End of Trump prosecutions renews scrutiny of DOJ's pace
item.description: Special counsel Jack Smith's move to dismiss Donald Trump's two federal indictments has sparked finger-pointing from those eager to see the president-elect held to account, with critics airing their frustrations at the Department of Justice (DOJ) and the courts for the anticlimactic end to the case.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, KGET 17, NBC
